original_hypothesis.title: Owner Can Drain User-Claimable TITN via Unrestricted withdraw()
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The withdraw() function allows the owner to transfer any amount of any ERC20 token from the MergeTgt contract to the owner's address with no restrictions. This includes TITN tokens that have been allocated to users (tracked in claimableTitnPerUser). The owner can call withdraw(titn, titn.balanceOf(address(this))) to drain all TITN, making claimTitn and withdrawRemainingTitn permanently revert for all users. This also corrupts the withdrawRemainingTitn snapshot if performed before the 360-day mark, as remainingTitnAfter1Year would be set to a deflated balance.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While this requires owner collusion (LOW realism), the severity is CRITICAL as it affects all user funds. The withdraw function has no amount limits, no token restrictions, and no checks against outstanding user claims. Combined with no timelock, the owner can act instantaneously.

proof_of_signal.pattern: withdraw() has no checks beyond onlyOwner: `token.safeTransfer(owner(), amount)` — can withdraw TITN that is owed to users. No check that amount <= (balance - totalTitnClaimable).

preconditions: Owner decides to withdraw TITN (trust model violation)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: withdraw() has no guard: `require(amount <= titn.balanceOf(this) - totalTitnClaimable)` is missing
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Owner extracts up to 173.7M TITN; all user claims revert permanently

economic_questions: Is there any governance or multisig protecting the owner key?
Can this be combined with the snapshot corruption for a more subtle attack?
What is the time-value window: can owner withdraw and re-deposit before users notice?
validation_requirements: Confirm owner can withdraw full TITN balance with no revert
Confirm claimTitn reverts after owner drains TITN
Confirm withdrawRemainingTitn underflows after owner drains TITN

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Near-infinite (gas cost vs total TITN value)

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After users have deposited TGT but before they claim TITN (maximizes extractable value)
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete protocol collapse; total user fund loss

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All users who deposited TGT and have not yet claimed TITN

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Owner address, multisig status, timelock presence
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: withdraw() lacks: amount guard against user-committed TITN, token-type restriction for TITN

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
